label: Matrix factorization & topic models

node.label: Matrix factorization & topic models
node.definition: Matrix factorization and topic models explain sparse user–item, word–document, or other observations through a smaller set of latent factors.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Matrix factorization & topic models is modeled as depending on Linear algebra, Latent variables & hidden state and feeding downstream nodes such as Recommendation, ranking & personalization.
examples: atlas downstream link to Recommendation, ranking & personalization
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Latent variables & hidden state.

dependencies.rationale: Matrix factorization & topic models rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
Matrix factorization & topic models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.
Matrix factorization & topic models follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.

In this atlas graph, Matrix factorization & topic models has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy because Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
